dasRennen.label: Oetz Trophy
dasRennen.body: The Ötztaler Ache. WW V. One of the hardest kayak races in the Alps. No open entry — you race here by invitation only. The course demands experience, technique, and the willingness to push your limits.

festivalOverview.label: 17–20 September 2026
festivalOverview.body: The Ötztal Kayak Festival brings Europe's paddling community together for four days in Oetz. Racing, demo boats, film screenings, live music — and of course, the Ötztaler Ache.

footer.tagline: Extreme Kayak Championships · Ötztaler Ache · Since 2019

impressum.label: Legal
impressum.title: Legal Notice
impressum.liabilityText: Our website contains links to the websites of third parties. As the content of these websites is not under our control, we cannot assume any liability for such external content. The respective provider of the linked websites is solely responsible for their content.

datenschutz.label: Legal
datenschutz.title: Privacy Policy
datenschutz.intro: We, Source To Sea GmbH, Natterer See 1, 6161 Natters, Austria only collect, process and use your personal data with your consent or based on another legal basis in accordance with the GDPR. The provision of your data is required for your participation in the OETZ TROPHY.
datenschutz.s1Text: We only collect personal data that is necessary for the implementation, evaluation and optimisation of the OETZ TROPHY or that you have voluntarily provided to us. In particular, the following data will be processed: name, address, email address, telephone number, date of birth, age, gender, nationality, placement, club/association, and video recordings and photos.
datenschutz.s2Text: As a participant in the OETZ TROPHY, you have the right at any time to information about your stored personal data, their origin and recipients and the purpose of the data processing, as well as the right to correction, data transfer, objection, restriction of processing and blocking or deletion of incorrect or inadmissibly processed data.
datenschutz.s3Text: Your personal data is protected by appropriate organisational and technical precautions. These precautions relate in particular to protection against unauthorised, illegal or accidental access, processing, loss, use and manipulation.
datenschutz.s4Text: We will not process the data provided to us for purposes other than what is necessary for your participation in the OETZ TROPHY or purposes covered by your consent. This does not include the use for statistical purposes, provided that the data provided has been anonymised.
datenschutz.s5Text: Your personal data will be processed by us and, under certain circumstances, by third parties. We share your data (in particular name, gender, age, nationality, club/association, placement, photos and video recordings) with online and print media, clubs/associations and other persons who have an interest in the OETZ TROPHY.
datenschutz.s6Text: We strive to ensure that data breaches are identified at an early stage and — should you be affected — reported to you or the competent supervisory authority, indicating the respective categories of data affected.
datenschutz.s7Text: We will not retain your personal data for longer than is necessary to fulfil our contractual or legal obligations and to defend against any liability claims.
datenschutz.s8Text: Our website only uses cookies that are necessary for the operation of the site, unless you give us further permission. Our website uses Google Analytics with the extension 'anonymizeIp' to ensure anonymisation of the IP address.

oetzTrophy.label: The Race
oetzTrophy.title: Oetz Trophy
oetzTrophy.intro: The OETZ TROPHY is one of the hardest kayak races in the Alps. On the Ötztaler Ache — whitewater class V — the world's best paddlers compete head to head. No open entry: you race here by invitation only.
oetzTrophy.courseText: The race course covers the most demanding sections of the Ötztaler Ache. Class V whitewater with technical passages, powerful hydraulics and fast currents. The course demands experience, technique, and the willingness to push your limits. Only those who know and respect the river stand a chance here.
oetzTrophy.rule1: Participation by personal invitation from the organisers only.
oetzTrophy.rule2: All participants must have proven experience in class IV–V whitewater.
oetzTrophy.rule3: Full safety equipment is mandatory: helmet, PFD, spray skirt, throw bag.
oetzTrophy.rule4: Instructions from safety personnel must be followed at all times.
oetzTrophy.rule5: Acceptance of the terms & conditions and privacy policy at bib number distribution.

boaterX.label: Boater Cross
boaterX.title: Boater X
boaterX.intro: The Oetz Kayak Cross — known as Boater X — is the open race on the Friday before the OETZ TROPHY. Anyone with sufficient whitewater experience can enter. Four paddlers start simultaneously, racing head to head through the rapids.
boaterX.formatText: Four paddlers start simultaneously on a set course. The first two advance — round after round, until only four finalists remain. Pure excitement for spectators and athletes alike. Fast decisions, close duels, full commitment.
boaterX.rule1: Open entry for all paddlers with whitewater experience (min. class III).
boaterX.rule3: Four paddlers per heat — the first two advance to the next round.
boaterX.rule4: Intentional body contact and obstruction of other paddlers leads to disqualification.

kajakfestival.label: Festival
kajakfestival.title: Kayak Festival
kajakfestival.intro: The Ötztal Kayak Festival is the heart of the weekend. For four days, paddlers from across Europe gather in Oetz — to paddle, celebrate and connect. Beyond the races, there are demo boats, film screenings, workshops and live music.
kajakfestival.day1Desc: Opening of the Kayak Festival. Arrivals, campsite setup, first community gathering. Welcome party in the evening.
kajakfestival.day2Desc: Boater X (Oetz Kayak Cross) — the open race. Demo boats from sponsors. Film screenings and live music in the evening.
kajakfestival.day3Desc: OETZ TROPHY — the main race (invite only). Award ceremony and evening programme.
kajakfestival.day4Desc: Free paddling, community runs on the Ötztaler Ache. Group farewell paddle and departures.
kajakfestival.locationText: The festival takes place in Oetz in the Ötztal valley, Tyrol. Camping is available directly at the festival grounds. For accommodation, we recommend the Ötztal Tourism portal. From Innsbruck, it's approximately 45 minutes by car.

registration.label: Registration
registration.title: Boater X Registration
registration.intro: Register for the Oetz Kayak Cross (Boater X). Open entry for all paddlers with whitewater experience (min. class III). The registration fee secures your start position.
registration.closedText: Registration for Boater X 2026 opens on 1 May 2026. Check back then!
registration.successText: Your registration for Boater X 2026 has been received and payment confirmed. You will receive a confirmation email. See you on the Ötztaler Ache!
